chat_messages.text:
Hey Claude.

Do you know how I can remove all empty space between a clip that I just deleted a segment from in iMovie? As in, to take the resulting second part of the video and slide it back to meet with the first part after clipping?

content.text:
Hey Claude.

Do you know how I can remove all empty space between a clip that I just deleted a segment from in iMovie? As in, to take the resulting second part of the video and slide it back to meet with the first part after clipping?

chat_messages.text:
The user is asking about a basic video editing operation in iMovie - how to remove the gap that's left after deleting a segment from the middle of a clip. This is a common editing task and I can answer this from my knowledge without needing to search, as this is stable software functionality that doesn't change frequently.

When you delete a segment from the middle of a clip in iMovie, it usually leaves a gap, and you want to close that gap by moving the remaining parts together. This is typically called "ripple delete" or closing gaps in video editing terminology.In iMovie, after you delete a segment from a clip, you can easily close the gap by using the "Close Gap" feature:

**Method 1 - Right-click method:**
1. Right-click on the empty space/gap left after your deletion
2. Select "Close Gap" from the context menu
3. The clips will automatically slide together

**Method 2 - Select and delete the gap:**
1. Click on the empty space to select it
2. Press the Delete key
3. The gap will close and clips will move together

**Method 3 - Drag to close:**
You can also manually drag the second part of your video to the left until it meets the first part, though the right-click method is usually faster and more precise.

The "Close Gap" feature is really handy because it not only moves your clips together but also maintains the timing and sync of any other tracks (like audio or titles) that might be running alongside your main video.

This should give you a seamless edit with no empty space between your clips!

content.text:
In iMovie, after you delete a segment from a clip, you can easily close the gap by using the "Close Gap" feature:

**Method 1 - Right-click method:**
1. Right-click on the empty space/gap left after your deletion
2. Select "Close Gap" from the context menu
3. The clips will automatically slide together

**Method 2 - Select and delete the gap:**
1. Click on the empty space to select it
2. Press the Delete key
3. The gap will close and clips will move together

**Method 3 - Drag to close:**
You can also manually drag the second part of your video to the left until it meets the first part, though the right-click method is usually faster and more precise.

The "Close Gap" feature is really handy because it not only moves your clips together but also maintains the timing and sync of any other tracks (like audio or titles) that might be running alongside your main video.

This should give you a seamless edit with no empty space between your clips!
